steps.title: Identify the passage purpose
steps.content: The text describes Henderson's study of late nineteenth- and early twentieth-century artifacts from a Texas agricultural and domestic site. It explains what she found about hook-and-eye closures and then states her conclusion about how Black female farmworkers balanced fashionable silhouettes with the demands of physical labor.

steps.title: Reject unsupported purposes
steps.content: Choice A invents an unexpected discovery about how quickly fashions changed. Choice B says the women resisted traditional gender ideals, but the text says they balanced hegemonic ideals with work requirements. Choice C says the text evaluates scholarship and focuses on urban fashion ideals, but the passage neutrally summarizes a study at an agricultural and domestic site.

steps.title: Match the summary purpose
steps.content: The passage's main action is to summarize research findings. Those findings explain factors that shaped a fashion practice: contemporary ideals of femininity and the physical demands of farmwork. That purpose matches the choice about a study of factors influencing a fashion practice among Black female farmworkers. The answer is D.
